meta.title: 4 Days Bali + Nusa Penida Island Tour from RM988 | Kelingking Beach & Angel Billabong | Dhesu
meta.description: Explore Bali and the dramatic island of Nusa Penida in 4 days from RM988. Visit Kelingking Beach, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach and Crystal Bay by fast boat, plus Ubud highlands, Tirta Empul temple, Uluwatu and Jimbaran Bay seafood dinner. Private tour, daily departures.
meta.keywords: Bali Nusa Penida tour, Kelingking Beach, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach, Crystal Bay, 4 days Bali, Nusa Penida island package, Bali island hopping, Tirta Empul temple, Bali Malaysia package RM988

hero.badge: Bali + Nusa Penida, Indonesia — 4 Days 3 Nights
hero.title: 4 Days Bali +
hero.intro: Add the wild, untamed beauty of Nusa Penida to your Bali adventure — T-Rex cliffs at Kelingking, the natural infinity pool of Angel Billabong, iconic Broken Beach and crystal-clear Crystal Bay, all reached by fast boat from Sanur.
hero.heroImageAlt: Dramatic Kelingking Beach cliff face shaped like a T-Rex on Nusa Penida island, Bali

quickFacts.saving: Save RM250 per person (20% online discount)
includes: Meals as specified (3 breakfasts, 2 lunches, 3 dinners)
excludes: ETIQA Travel Insurance Area 2 Gold RM41
Optional activities (Bali Swing RM68, candlelight dinner RM90)

overview.heading: Bali's Cultural Heart Meets Nusa Penida's Wild Island Beauty
body: Nusa Penida has emerged as one of Indonesia's most spectacular travel discoveries — an island of dramatic sea cliffs, hidden coves and crystal-clear waters just a 30-minute fast boat ride from Bali's eastern shore. This 4-day package combines the best of both worlds: Bali's iconic cultural experiences on Days 1 and 3, and a full day devoted to exploring Nusa Penida's most extraordinary natural landmarks on Day 2.

From the T-Rex-shaped cliff face at Kelingking Beach — voted one of the most beautiful places in all of Indonesia — to the natural infinity tide pool of Angel Billabong, the perfectly curved horseshoe cove of Broken Beach and the azure diving haven of Crystal Bay, Nusa Penida delivers drama and beauty at every turn. Combined with Uluwatu's clifftop temple, the sacred Tirta Empul spring, Ubud's cultural villages and a legendary Jimbaran Bay seafood dinner, this is the definitive Bali island-and-mainland adventure.

items.title: Kelingking Beach — One of the Most Beautiful Places in Indonesia
items.body: The iconic T-Rex-shaped cliff face of Kelingking is Nusa Penida's most famous landmark and one of the most photographed viewpoints in all of Indonesia. Stand at the clifftop lookout and gaze down at the pristine white sand beach and electric-blue water hundreds of metres below — a view of almost otherworldly beauty.
items.imageAlt: Iconic T-Rex cliff face at Kelingking Beach viewpoint on Nusa Penida island, Bali

items.title: Angel Billabong & Broken Beach
items.body: Angel Billabong is a natural infinity tidal pool carved into the coastal rock where crystal-clear turquoise seawater cascades in mesmerising pools against the sea backdrop. Directly adjacent, Broken Beach is a perfectly round horseshoe cove with its arch eroded away, creating a dramatic natural sea bridge framing the emerald ocean within.
items.imageAlt: Angel Billabong natural infinity tide pool on Nusa Penida island, Bali Indonesia

items.title: Crystal Bay — Snorkelling Paradise
items.body: With its crystal-clear waters, vibrant coral gardens and abundant marine life including turtles and Mola Mola (sunfish in season), Crystal Bay is one of the finest snorkelling and diving sites in all of Nusa Penida. Even from the shore, the water's extraordinary clarity is breathtaking.
items.imageAlt: Crystal Bay with clear turquoise water and coral reefs on Nusa Penida island

items.title: Uluwatu Cliff Temple & Kecak Fire Dance
items.body: Begin your Bali adventure on the edge of a 70-metre sea cliff at the sacred Uluwatu Temple, then watch the mesmerising Kecak Fire Dance at sunset — 50 performers chanting and dancing around a flaming torch as the Ramayana story unfolds against the dying light over the Indian Ocean.
items.imageAlt: Uluwatu cliff temple above the Indian Ocean with dramatic sunset sky, Bali

items.title: Tirta Empul Sacred Holy Spring Temple
items.body: Visit the thousand-year-old Tirta Empul temple complex in Ubud, built around sacred spring waters believed to have purifying and healing powers. Watch Balinese devotees performing ritual bathing in the spring's crystal pools, or participate yourself in a guided purification ceremony.
items.imageAlt: Sacred Tirta Empul holy spring temple with ornate Balinese architecture in Ubud

items.title: Jimbaran Bay Grilled Seafood Dinner
items.body: No Bali trip is complete without dinner at Jimbaran Bay — fresh grilled seafood served beachside as the sun sets over the Indian Ocean. Prawns, fish, squid, lobster and crab, cooked over coconut husks and paired with rice, vegetables and Balinese sambal, make for an extraordinary final memory of the island.
items.imageAlt: Grilled seafood dinner laid on tables at Jimbaran Bay beach at sunset, Bali

days.title: Melasti Beach, Uluwatu, Kecak Fire Dance & Jimbaran Bay
days.description: Your adventure begins with a warm airport greeting and hotel transfer. Once checked in, head to the beautiful Melasti Beach — a scenic coastal gem framed by dramatic limestone cliffs with pristine white sand and clear turquoise water. As the afternoon golden hour approaches, make your way to Uluwatu Temple, one of Bali's most sacred sea temples, perched dramatically on a cliff face 70 metres above crashing ocean waves. The clifftop walk rewards you with extraordinary ocean views before the highlight: the FREE Kecak Fire Dance performance at sunset, featuring over 50 performers chanting 'cak' in interlocking rhythms as they enact scenes from the Ramayana epic by flickering firelight. As dusk deepens, head down to Jimbaran Bay for a legendary grilled BBQ seafood dinner on the beach — prawns, squid, fish and lobster cooked to perfection over coconut husks, with the sound of waves as your background music.
suggestedActivities: Melasti Beach — white sand cove with dramatic cliffs
Uluwatu Temple — clifftop walk and sea temple
FREE Kecak Fire Dance at sunset
Grilled BBQ seafood dinner at Jimbaran Bay
Optional: Romantic candlelight dinner with floral decor (RM90)

days.title: Fast Boat to Nusa Penida — Kelingking, Angel Billabong, Broken Beach & Crystal Bay
days.description: The most thrilling day of your trip begins early with breakfast before departing from Sanur Beach on a fast boat to Toyapakeh port on Nusa Penida. Once on the island, your local guide takes you to four of Southeast Asia's most extraordinary natural sites. First is Angel Billabong, a natural infinity tidal pool of ethereal clarity carved into the coastal rock, where the ocean flows through to create cascading crystal pools. Adjacent is Broken Beach — a dramatic horseshoe-shaped cove where the cliff arch has eroded away to frame the vivid turquoise ocean within a perfect natural circle. Next comes the moment that stops everyone in their tracks: the Kelingking Beach viewpoint, where a T-Rex-shaped cliff towers above one of the most beautiful hidden beaches in all of Indonesia, the emerald water far below visible through crystalline air. Finally, Crystal Bay offers the island's finest snorkelling and diving, with abundant marine life and the remarkable chance to spot giant Mola Mola ocean sunfish in season. Return by fast boat to Bali for dinner at Fork Bakery & Grill.
suggestedActivities: Fast boat departure from Sanur Beach to Nusa Penida (Toyapakeh)
Angel Billabong — natural infinity tidal pool
Broken Beach — horseshoe sea arch cove
Kelingking Beach — iconic T-Rex clifftop viewpoint
Crystal Bay — snorkelling and marine life
Fast boat return to Bali
Dinner at Fork Bakery & Grill

days.title: Ubud Highlands, Mount Batur Volcano Views & Tirta Empul Temple
days.description: Day 3 takes you deep into Bali's cool and culturally rich Ubud highlands. Begin at Celuk Village for a look at master goldsmiths and silversmiths at work, then explore the magnificent Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung) with its intricate carvings and traditional Balinese courtyard architecture. Browse the colourful Ubud Traditional Market for silk scarves, wooden carvings and handmade souvenirs. Visit the Tegalalang Rice Terrace — one of Asia's most photographed landscapes — with its cascading emerald terraces filling a steep jungle valley. An optional Alas Arum Bali Swing (RM68) provides breathtaking aerial views. Stop at a local coffee plantation for Luwak coffee and herbal tea tastings before the spectacular Indonesian buffet lunch at Grand Puncak Sari on the rim of the Kintamani caldera, overlooking the active Mount Batur volcano and Lake Batur far below. The afternoon spiritual highlight is Tirta Empul Temple, where sacred spring waters have been used for Balinese Hindu purification rituals for over a millennium. Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta before returning to the hotel.
suggestedActivities: Celuk Gold & Silver Village
Ubud Royal Palace (Puri Saren Agung)
Ubud Traditional Market
Tegalalang Rice Terrace
Optional: Alas Arum Bali Swing (RM68, self-paid)
Local coffee plantation — Luwak coffee tasting
Kintamani volcano panorama lunch at Grand Puncak Sari
Tirta Empul Sacred Holy Spring Temple
Dinner at Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta

days.title: Free Morning & Departure
days.description: Your final Bali morning is completely free — enjoy the hotel pool, take a beach stroll, browse a local market or treat yourself to a traditional Balinese massage. When it's time, check out of your hotel, stop briefly at a souvenir shop for last-minute gifts — quality Balinese coffee, batik, silver jewellery and local handicrafts make wonderful keepsakes — before your smooth airport transfer to Ngurah Rai International Airport.
suggestedActivities: Free morning — hotel pool, beach or spa
Hotel checkout
Souvenir shop visit
Airport transfer for departure

whyBook.heading: Why Book Your Bali + Nusa Penida Tour Through Dhesu
whyBook.body: Dhesu Travel has been crafting exceptional Bali holidays for Malaysian travellers since 1988. Our Nusa Penida add-on is one of the most-requested upgrades to our Bali packages — and with good reason. We handle all the fast boat logistics, island transfers and local guide arrangements seamlessly, so you can just enjoy the extraordinary scenery.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed and registered with MATTA and IATA, Dhesu provides complete consumer protection for every booking, giving you financial security and professional service.

points.title: Trusted Since 1988
points.body: Over 35 years of organising island holidays for Malaysian travellers gives us the depth of local knowledge, vetted guides and supplier reliability to deliver a flawless experience.

points.title: 100% Private Tours
points.body: Your own vehicle, your own guide, your own fast boat. Nusa Penida is explored on your terms — no crowded group tours or rigid timetables.

points.title: Seamless Island Logistics
points.body: We handle all fast boat bookings, island transfers and local guide arrangements between Bali and Nusa Penida — you simply show up and enjoy the ride.

sections.heading: Why Nusa Penida is Bali's Most Exciting Day Trip
sections.body: Nusa Penida remained largely undiscovered by international travellers until the early 2010s, but once the world saw the extraordinary images from Kelingking Beach, Angel Billabong and Broken Beach, it quickly became one of Indonesia's hottest destinations. Unlike Bali's more developed tourist areas, Nusa Penida retains a raw, wild beauty — dramatic cliffs, rough roads, hidden beaches — that makes it feel like genuine discovery even today. The island is best experienced as part of a Bali package rather than independently, as the logistics of fast boats, local jeeps and island navigation are considerably easier when handled by an experienced guide.

sections.heading: Best Time to Visit Nusa Penida from Bali
sections.body: The fast boat crossing from Sanur to Nusa Penida (approximately 30–45 minutes) is most comfortable during Bali's dry season from April to October, when sea conditions are calm and the sky is clear for photography. The wet season (November–March) can see rougher crossings on some days, though trips are rarely cancelled entirely. For the best Mola Mola (ocean sunfish) sightings at Crystal Bay, plan your visit between July and October when these extraordinary creatures migrate to the shallower waters.

sections.heading: Combining Nusa Penida with the Best of Bali
sections.body: This package is intelligently structured so that Nusa Penida occupies Day 2, sandwiched between the beach and temple experiences of Day 1 and the Ubud highland cultural tour of Day 3. This means you experience both the dramatic island landscapes and Bali's rich cultural heartland without any logistical complexity. It is the most efficient and satisfying way to combine both destinations in a single 4-day trip.

items.question: How do we get to Nusa Penida?
items.answer: You travel by fast boat from Sanur Beach on Bali's eastern coast, departing in the morning. The crossing takes approximately 30–45 minutes and arrives at Toyapakeh port on Nusa Penida. All fast boat tickets and transfers are included in your package.

items.question: Is Nusa Penida suitable for people who get seasick?
items.answer: The Sanur–Nusa Penida crossing can be choppy, particularly between November and March. If you are prone to motion sickness, we recommend taking seasickness tablets before departure. During the dry season (April–October), the crossing is generally calm and comfortable.

items.question: Can we swim at Kelingking Beach?
items.answer: The descent to Kelingking Beach itself is very steep and challenging (approximately 30–45 minutes each way on a rough path), and the beach has strong currents making swimming dangerous. Most visitors enjoy the beach from the famous clifftop viewpoint, which delivers one of the most stunning views in Indonesia.

items.question: What meals are included?
items.answer: Day 1: Dinner (Jimbaran Bay BBQ seafood). Day 2: Breakfast, lunch on Nusa Penida, dinner (Fork Bakery & Grill). Day 3: Breakfast, lunch (Kintamani volcano viewpoint), dinner (Fork Bakery and Grill Kuta). Day 4: Breakfast. Total: 3 breakfasts, 2 lunches, 3 dinners.

items.question: What is the best camera gear for Nusa Penida?
items.answer: A wide-angle lens is ideal for capturing the sweeping cliff panoramas at Kelingking and Broken Beach. For Crystal Bay snorkelling, an underwater camera or GoPro with a waterproof case is excellent. Bring a fully charged power bank as the island has limited charging facilities.
